skill.name: github-pr-best-practices
skill.description: Guides AI assistants to create clear GitHub pull request titles, descriptions, test plans, and GitHub CLI commands using Conventional Commits.
skill.summary: Create GitHub pull requests with Conventional Commit titles, structured descriptions, and bilingual templates.
skill.author: 1natsu172

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found documentation-only content. The command examples are legitimate Git and GitHub CLI usage for creating pull requests, and the URLs are documentation or attribution links. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, credential access, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Legitimate GitHub CLI Commands Can Affect Remote Repositories
low_findings.description: The skill includes examples for gh pr create, gh pr edit, gh pr merge, and related GitHub CLI commands. These are appropriate for the skill purpose, but they can create or modify pull requests when an assistant executes them for a user.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly presented as GitHub PR workflow examples. They are not malicious, but they can perform external GitHub actions when used.

low_findings.title: Static Malware Indicators Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography, reconnaissance, shell substitution, and high-entropy findings occur in Markdown examples, PR template text, or ordinary URLs. No executable malware behavior or obfuscated payload was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms the flagged files are documentation files. The suspicious tokens are examples, headings, links, and template content rather than runnable code.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution in GitHub CLI Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill demonstrates a HEREDOC passed to gh pr create through shell command substitution. The example quotes EOF to prevent variable expansion, but assistants should avoid inserting untrusted text into shell commands without review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell pattern is real, but it is contained in documentation and uses a safer quoted HEREDOC form. Risk depends on how an assistant applies the example.

content.value_statement: Poor pull request descriptions slow reviews and hide the intent of a change. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce clear PR titles, summaries, and test plans.
actual_capabilities: Formats pull request titles with Conventional Commit types and optional scopes.
Builds structured PR descriptions with summary and test plan sections.
Supports English and Japanese PR content patterns.
Adapts output to existing project pull request templates.
Provides GitHub CLI examples for draft and ready pull requests.
Lists common PR mistakes and preferred corrections.
limitations: Does not inspect code changes unless the assistant reads the repository separately.
Does not guarantee that tests listed in a PR were actually run.
May need project-specific conventions beyond the included templates.
GitHub CLI commands require user authorization and repository access.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Feature PR
use_cases.description: Turn a feature branch into a draft pull request with a conventional title, concise summary, and practical test plan.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Review Text
use_cases.description: Give a team consistent PR sections, language rules, and examples for common change types.

use_cases.title: Write Bilingual PRs
use_cases.description: Generate English or Japanese PR descriptions while preserving the same review structure.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic PR
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single change
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a GitHub pull request title and description for this change: [describe the change]. Use English and include a test plan.

prompt_templates.title: Use Conventional Commits
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with scope and type guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a PR for these commits: [paste commit summaries]. Choose the best Conventional Commit type and scope.

prompt_templates.title: Follow Project Template
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request using an existing template
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the project pull request template below and fill it for this branch. Preserve every section header. Template: [paste template]. Changes: [paste summary].

prompt_templates.title: Create With GitHub CLI
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a safe gh pr create command for this branch. Use a draft PR, quoted heredoc body, Conventional Commit title, and a complete test plan.

output_examples.input: A branch adds OAuth login and token refresh tests.
output_examples.output: Title: feat(auth): add OAuth login. Output includes a summary of authentication changes and a test plan for login, refresh, and error cases.

output_examples.input: A bug fix resolves a race condition in session handling.
output_examples.output: Title: fix(session): prevent concurrent state updates. Output explains the user impact, root cause, and regression tests.

output_examples.input: A documentation update needs Japanese PR text.
output_examples.output: Output uses Japanese section labels, concise change bullets, and review checks for accuracy, links, and examples.

best_practices: Give the assistant the branch purpose, changed files, and test results before generating PR text.
Review generated GitHub CLI commands before running them against a remote repository.
Use the repository pull request template when one exists.
anti_patterns: Do not claim tests passed unless they were actually run or verified.
Do not mix English and Japanese sections in the same PR unless the project requires it.
Do not replace project-required template sections with a generic PR body.

faq.question: Does this skill create pull requests automatically?
faq.answer: It provides PR content and GitHub CLI guidance. The assistant or user must still run any command intentionally.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance is Markdown-based and can be used by Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it support Japanese pull request descriptions?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Japanese section labels, examples, and phrase guidance.

faq.question: Will it read my repository changes?
faq.answer: Only if the assistant using the skill is given repository access and reads the relevant files or commits.

faq.question: Does it enforce Conventional Commits?
faq.answer: It guides the assistant to use Conventional Commit types, scopes, and descriptions for PR titles.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed files are documentation-only, with low operational risk from legitimate GitHub CLI examples.
